privileged_info_summary: All three SCOTUS cases have been independently verified as real historical Supreme Court cases with accurate metadata. Gideon v. Wainwright (372 U.S. 335, decided March 18, 1963) was a unanimous 9-0 decision with Justice Black as the opinion author. Miranda v. Arizona (384 U.S. 436, decided June 13, 1966) was decided 5-4 with Chief Justice Warren as the opinion author. Tinker v. Des Moines (393 U.S. 503, decided February 24, 1969) was decided 7-2 with Justice Fortas as the opinion author. All four required legal research domains (oyez.org, justia.com, law.cornell.edu, supremecourt.gov) are legitimate, authoritative sources for Supreme Court case law. The data is not synthetic but based on real historical legal records.
